role_summary: Nation of Islam national spokesperson from 1957 to his March 1964 break with the organization; founder of the Muslim Mosque Inc and the Organization of Afro-American Unity (OAAU) in 1964; assassinated February 21, 1965.
ai_reading: Five corpus voices discuss Malcolm X: William Lamar Strickland (entry 127), Ekwueme Michael Thelwell (entry 34, SNCC Washington office director who edited Carmichael's memoir), John Carlos (entry 62, 1968 Olympic Black Power salute), John Churchville (entry 63), and William S. Leventhal (entry 129). The cluster is tightly Black Power and athlete-activist; the embedding does not surface Malcolm X in the SCLC or NAACP transcripts the catalog separately tracks, consistent with the cultural placement of his vocabulary as outside the King-era civil rights mainstream.
biographical_paragraph: Malcolm X is discussed in this corpus by 5 interviewees across the catalog (William Lamar Strickland (entry 127), Ekwueme Michael Thelwell (entry 34), John Carlos (entry 62), John Churchville (entry 63), William S. Leventhal (entry 129)), but was not interviewed for the Civil Rights History Project; this catalog page treats Malcolm X as an external figure whose presence in the archive is mediated through other voices. The canonical scholarly source the catalog cites for biographical orientation appears as [src: 1] below.

sources.title: Manning Marable, Malcolm X: A Life of Reinvention
